# IGBundle — Fiber Bundle Adapters for Language Models

## Project Overview
IGBundle is a research project that applies fiber bundle geometry to transformer language models.
A geometric adapter at a single transformer layer (Layer 12 of Qwen 2.5-7B) introduces Poincare
ball coordinates, categorical fiber sections, Hamiltonian dynamics, and Riemannian curvature
regularization as an inductive bias for hierarchical reasoning.

## Team
- Jesus Vilela Jato — Principal Researcher & Orchestrator
- Claude (Anthropic) — Geometry, diagnostics, training pipeline, GSP, NMEM
- Gemini (Google) — UI, memory backend, evaluation, dashboard

## Key Documentation
- Thesis: /docs/wiki.html
- Get Started: /docs/demo.html
- Source: https://github.com/jesusvilela/IGBundle-LLM
- Model: https://huggingface.co/jesusvilela/igbundle-qwen2.5-7b-riemannian

## Architecture
- Base Model: Qwen 2.5-7B (NF4 quantized via Unsloth)
- Adapter: GeometricIGBundleAdapter at Layer 12 (~0.9% params added)
- Base Manifold: Poincare Ball H^64 (D=64, curvature c=1)
- Fibers: K=16 categorical sections, P=8 mixture components
- Dynamics: Hamiltonian system with symplectic integration, Lorentz limiter (c=5.0)
- Curvature: Log-determinant Laplacian estimator, regularized toward kappa=-1
- Inference: GSP (Geometric Steering Probe) — GENERIC-compliant homeostatic controller
- Memory: NMEM (neuromorphic) with FHN decay, FFT spectral forgetting, Gabor interference
- UI: Neural Glass (Gradio) with real-time curvature/entropy telemetry

## Key Results
- Manifold Faithfulness: 6/6 tests PASS (K=-5.63, S=0.95, JSD=0.424, PT=0.041)
- Benchmarks preserved: ARC 54.86%, TruthfulQA 64.78%, Winogrande 71.03%, GSM8K 75.51%
- Geometry is real and measurable, not decorative
- Overhead: +15% training time, +4% inference latency, -30% convergence steps

## Quick Start
```bash
git clone https://github.com/jesusvilela/IGBundle-LLM
cd IGBundle-LLM
pip install -r requirements.txt
python train.py --config configs/igbundle_standard.yaml
```

## Source Structure
src/igbundle/
  geometry/      — Riemannian, hyperbolic, Poincare, KAN manifold
  modules/       — Geometric adapter, losses, attention, vision
  dynamics/      — Hamiltonian, FitzHugh-Nagumo, equilibrium propagation
  fibers/        — Fiber state, constraints, swarm executor
  steering/      — GSP controller
  optimization/  — Symplectic optimizer, SPIDER variance reduction
  training/      — Geometric trainer, GRPO, losses
